items.description: The 2026 Greatest American City Sweep was a huge success thanks to all the amazing community volunteers, staff, and neighbors who came out on Saturday to help clean up litter around our neighborhoods and city streets. We picked up over 120 bags of trash in just a few short hours, and volunteer enjoyed free breakfast & lunch plus some nice shirts thanks to our event sponsors, Duke Energy, PalmettoPride, The Beacon, and Keep OneSpartanburg Beautiful! 🚮

items.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday rejected a push by President Donald Trump to throw out a jury’s $5 million finding that he sexually abused the writer E. Jean Carroll at a New York City department store in the mid-1990s and later defamed her. The high court declined to take up the case [...]

items.title: Crews respond to McCormick Co. church fire
items.description: MCCORMICK COUNTY, S.C. (WSPA) -- Crews from the Upstate were called to fight a fire over the weekend in McCormick County. The fire started Sunday morning, just after 10 a.m., at Saint Goodwill Baptist Church on Highway 28, which is south of Abbeville. Abbeville County Fire Services said several departments, including Greenwood County, responded. Firefighters [...]

items.title: Supreme Court halts Trump's firing of Fed's Lisa Cook
items.description: The Supreme Court blocked President Trump’s firing of Federal Reserve board member Lisa Cook on Monday in a 5-4 vote, ruling she can remain on the job as her legal challenge moves forward.   Trump became the first president to attempt to fire a Fed board member in its 112-year history when he sacked Cook last year over accusations of mortgage [...]

The coroner's office was notified about the [...]

items.title: I absolutely love the chopped
items.description: I absolutely love the chopped cheese sandwich at Big Apple Cafe! I get it without lettuce and tomato. Decided to add my own tabasco peppers to it for an extra kick. items.title: Supreme Court rules states can accept mail ballots after Election Day
items.description: The Supreme Court on Monday ruled 5-4 that states can accept mail ballots after polls close in federal races, rejecting the Republican National Committee’s (RNC) push to defeat the practice. More than a dozen states deem mail ballots that arrive after Election Day valid, so long as they were postmarked by then. Justice Amy Coney [...]

items.description: Former NFL running back Chris Johnson announced he has been diagnosed with ALS, also known as Lou Gehrig's disease.

items.title: No budget, no raises: July 1 pay bumps likely delayed for NC teachers, state employees and retirees
items.description: With just days until the start of North Carolina's new fiscal year, thousands of state employees and retirees are still waiting to learn when expected raises and bonuses will arrive as lawmakers continue negotiating a state budget.

items.title: Did you feel it? Minor earthquake reported in Union Co.
items.description: CROSS ANCHOR, S.C. (WSPA) -- A minor earthquake occurred in Union County Tuesday night, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. items.title: Judge sets April court date for Alex Murdaugh retrial
items.description: LEXINGTON COUNTY, S.C. (WCBD) – A judge has tentatively set an April 2027 retrial date for convicted killer Alex Murdaugh following a status hearing Monday in Lexington County. items.description: Cultural reclamation in the making, the ugly and uncomfortable history and or origin of Native American “Fry Bread” is not mine to tell, but it is there and can’t be ignored. A true Catawba Nation, family owned and operated, food truck. If Bison (Yada), ground Beef & pinto beans or Chicken and green chile, “Indian Tacos” isn’t on your list… Strawberries and Cream fry bread surely is…. items.description: Work to build and scale a small business ecosystem continued thanks to Power Up Spartanburg, an initiative of Spartanburg County and OneSpartanburg, Inc. Power Up Spartanburg has engaged with more than 2,835 current and potential small business-owners, resulting in the creation of 264 jobs and 56 new businesses. items.description: Downtown Spartanburg is more than shops, streets, and buildings—it's a canvas. From vibrant colors to powerful stories painted on walls, our downtown murals celebrate the culture, history, and talent. One of our favorites can be found along Dunbar Street, adding color and character to the heart of our city. Support the arts that helps make Spartanburg a vibrant place to live, work, and visit.

On top of that, efforts to diversify the projects recruited to our community to bring more knowledge-based jobs to Spartanburg found increased success. items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: This sounds way better than the office building that was proposed. Honestly, I would  like to see a mixed use where the ground floor is shop/restaurant space, then a coulple of office floors with the Home2Suites on the upper levels.

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: Would be great to see something new come to this area of downtown. If memory serves me correctly, part of the deal Johnson has with the city is that for 3? (unsure of exact number) years after the construction of the hotel he is currently building no other new downtown hotels can receive tax breaks.

items.title: Downtown Projects & Developments
items.description: Pretty big news: at the July 7 DRB meeting, there will be a request for Preliminary Conceptual Approval for a Home2 Suites at 234 North Church Street.  This is the site where Montgomery Development proposed a 3-story office building several years ago.  The developer is Pinnacle Partnership (the ones who have done most of the hotels by the mall) and the applicant is RAD Architecture (possibly this firm).  It will be interesting to see the details of the project.

Join us, along with Chris Ceej Jefferson  and DJ Traxxondekk, in Downtown Spartanburg at 6 PM on South Daniel Morgan Avenue as we celebrate freedom, culture, and community with live music, food trucks, vendors, and more! 🎆 Bring your family and friends and be part of this special Juneteenth Celebration Weekend event. We can't wait to celebrate with you! As a reminder, free parking is available at the stadium garage.

items.title: This week we got together as a group named "PedalBurg" to bike around the city of Spartanburg! Biking in larger numbers ...
items.description:
This week we got together as a group named "PedalBurg" to bike around the city of Spartanburg! 
Biking in larger numbers is safer and it helps send a message to the city: we want to bike!

With biking infrastructure steadily growing around the Burg, people are able to utilize this method of transportation more often and more safely. We'd love to see even more bike friendly roads continue to be adopted 🚲

#strongtowns #strongtownsspartanburg #pedalburg #biking

Don't miss tonight's Juneteenth Celebration Week Block Party taking place from 6 PM-10 PM on S Daniel Morgan Ave. across from Fifth Third Park!

items.description: A new stay has arrived in Downtown Spartanburg! 🏨✨ We’re excited to welcome Fairfield Inn & Suites to our Downtown community! Whether you’re here for work, a concert, a weekend getaway, or to explore everything Spartanburg has to offer, Fairfield Inn is ready to welcome you with comfortable accommodations and top notch service. If you’re planning a visit to Spartanburg, be sure to keep them in mind for your next stay. Welcome to the neighborhood! 🎉 #ExperienceSpartanburg #DowntownSpartanburg

items.title: One of our favorite summer traditions! ☀️ Last Friday, we gathered for our annual company beach day. From lunch at The @...
items.description:
One of our favorite summer traditions! ☀️
 
Last Friday, we gathered for our annual company beach day. From lunch at The @windjammeriop to an afternoon on the beach, it was the perfect way to kick off the weekend.
 
A few out-of-town folks stayed a little longer, enjoying some extra beach time and a Mt. Joy concert before heading home. 🎶🏖️

#swpeople #swculture #beachday

items.title: We’ve done City budget podcasts before, but this year City Manager Chris Story joins us for a conversation less about wh...
items.description: We’ve done City budget podcasts before, but this year City Manager Chris Story joins us for a conversation less about what's in the budget and more about why it looks the way it does. On today’s podcast, we’re getting the story behind the numbers, and the thinking behind the choices: https://www.cityofspartanburg.org/m/newsfla…/home/detail/106.

items.title: Join Mayor Jerome Rice for a special Juneteenth edition of Miles with the Mayor this Saturday, June 20th at 8 AM! Starti...
items.description: Join Mayor Jerome Rice for a special Juneteenth edition of Miles with the Mayor this Saturday, June 20th at 8 AM! Starting at the Southside Cultural Monument, Mayor Rice's Juneteenth community walk celebrates history, culture, and unity in Spartanburg. Lace up your walking shoes and join the youth from our various community programs as we take steps toward remembering our past and building our future.

items.title: Thank you everyone for coming to the first PedalBurg! An event where we all get together and ride our bikes around the c...
items.description:
Thank you everyone for coming to the first PedalBurg! An event where we all get together and ride our bikes around the city 🚲 It was wonderful getting together as a community to enjoy the summer evening and we hope for many more in the future! 

A special thank you to @thewalkingspartan for leading the ride and for @es_ca_pi_st for taking these amazing photos 📸

#strongtowns #strongtownsburg #strongtownsspartanburg #pedalburg

items.title: The Land, the Home, and What Lasts We think about legacy a lot in this work. Not just the legacy of a beautiful home, bu...
items.description: The Land, the Home, and What Lasts We think about legacy a lot in this work. Not just the legacy of a beautiful home, but the legacy of how it sits on its land. Whether it depletes the resources around it or replenishes them. Whether it creates a connection to place that deepens over time. Native landscaping eliminates most irrigation demand and restores habitat for the wildlife that belongs there. Local materials reduce the energy cost of transportation and invest in regional craftspeople. Smar

items.title: Six years ago this week, on June 20, 2020, the day after Juneteenth, sixteen artists spent a single day painting “Black...
items.description: Six years ago this week, on June 20, 2020, the day after Juneteenth, sixteen artists spent a single day painting “Black Lives Matter” on W Broad St. in front of Spartanburg City Hall.  It became the first mural of its kind in South Carolina, but as those who created it will tell you, the paint was only part of the story. The day itself was just as important, bringing our community together and providing deep local meaning for the people of Spartanburg. W Broad St. is changing now, and the mural'

items.title: Taking grading beyond the screen. 🚧 Our first Grading Workshop brought team members from our Charlotte, Greenville, and...
items.description:
Taking grading beyond the screen. 🚧
 
Our first Grading Workshop brought team members from our Charlotte, Greenville, and Mount Pleasant offices together to connect design intent to real-world field conditions through 3D modeling exercises and hands-on demonstrations. Team members even took the lesson outside, using string lines to model grading concepts in the field and better understand how design decisions shape site conditions.
 
A great two days of collaboration, problem-solving, and knowle

items.title: The Palmetto Stop Is State Law in SC
items.description: On May 18th, 2026, South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster (R) signed into law S.812 , or the Stop-as-Yield Law for Bicycles. This makes South Carolina the 14th U.S. state to pass a version of the “Idaho Stop”, with Palmetto Walk Bike dubbing ours the “Palmetto Stop.” This is an exciting development for cycling safety in our state and enjoyed bipartisan support. The bill was introduced by Senator Luke A. Rankin (R-Horry), cosponsored by Senator Overture Walker (D-Richland), and supported in commi

Passive cooling and heating that reduces your energy bill and your reliance on mechanical systems. Materials that don’t off-gas harmful compounds into the air your family breathes. These aren’t luxury features. They’re good design. We designed South Carolina’s first LEED Platinum building, and we’ve been applying that same integrat

items.title: A beautiful evening spent slowing down, creating, and connecting 🌸💐 Our Charlotte Women’s Initiative recently enjoyed ...
items.description:
A beautiful evening spent slowing down, creating, and connecting 🌸💐
 
Our Charlotte Women’s Initiative recently enjoyed a Garden Gathering, where we created our own bouquets and enjoyed a guided meditation. It was the perfect reminder to pause, be present, and enjoy time together.
 
Thank you to @alfredwilliams_carolinas for hosting us, and to Jess from @gatherflowerstudio for sharing her creativity and helping make the evening so special.

#SWculture #SWpeople #womensinitiative

items.title: A building doesn’t appear overnight. It starts with a vision, a piece of property, and a whole lot of work that most peo...
items.description:
A building doesn’t appear overnight.

It starts with a vision, a piece of property, and a whole lot of work that most people never see.

Last week, we reached another important milestone at @christchurchanglicanmtp with the completion of the slab.

The groundwork has been laid. The foundation is in place. And soon, what has existed on paper for months will begin rising above the site for everyone to see.

These are the moments we love in construction. Watching an idea become something real.

items.title: Happy National Trails Day! 🌳 Trails shape the way people move through and experience a community, creating space for co...
items.description:
Happy National Trails Day! 🌳

Trails shape the way people move through and experience a community, creating space for connection, exploration, and everyday access to the outdoors. We’re grateful to help support spaces like these and the role they play in our communities.

What do you enjoy most when you’re on a trail? 🥾
 
#NationalTrailsDay #SWprojects #Trails #CommunityDesign

items.title: OneSpartanburg, Inc. is not affiliated with any group or funding effort attempting to influence County Council elections...
items.description: OneSpartanburg, Inc. is not affiliated with any group or funding effort attempting to influence County Council elections. Claims by candidates that we are linked to spending behind recent mailers, and that we invited candidates to meet with our investors, are false. We do aim to educate our constituents and partners on local candidates’ views on economic issues. We invited candidates to speak to municipal leaders at our regularly scheduled Mayoral meeting, and invited candidates to respond to a

items.description: One year and ten days ago, Johnston Design Group and Edifice were hired to design and build a new stadium for Triumph SC. Three weeks later, graders were already on site. And less than a year after that — we were there for the first game, the first roar of the crowd, and the first victory in their new home. The parking deck has reached the central stair/elevator tower that will have pedestrian access to the interior plaza, and you can see the center car entrance on Spring too.

items.description: Every day, most of us reserve a little time for ourselves. A quiet cup of coffee. A walk. A moment to breathe. What if your home did that for you — all the time? That's the idea behind Living Wellness in restorative design. Not wellness as a checklist, but as something built into the way a home is made: the quality of its light, the freshness of its air, its relationship to the land it sits on, the peace that comes from a space designed with you — your life, your rhythms, your wellbeing — in min

items.description: You’re invited to join the EdRedesign Lab at the Harvard Graduate School of Education for the virtual premiere of the latest episode of their series, DREAM ON, featuring the transformation of Spartanburg’s Northside. The powerful story shows how a cross-sector coalition of residents, local government, school districts, the Spartanburg Academic Movement and StriveTogether, and Purpose Built Communities helped transform the Northside. items.description: Our MPS Charlotte team once again participated in a successful CANstruction event and partnered with The Whiting Turner Contracting Company to  successfully design and build "Cow Abducted by UFO" an 8 ft tall UFO beaming up a cow out of around 2000 cans! Together, with our partners, we also raised $6734 to benefit Second Harvest Food Bank of Metrolina Our team took home two awards: Biggest Impact (for largest total donation) & Structural Ingenuity. What does it take to build a high school designed for the future, and make it work for students today? In this episode o...
items.description: What does it take to build a high school designed for the future, and make it work for students today? In this episode of The Future of K-12 Education, Les Tillery talks with Matt Massey, President of the Alabama School of Cyber Technology and Engineering (ASCTE). items.title: Projects like this are the heartbeat of our firm. We’re incredibly honored to have been awarded the Georgetown Port Rede...
items.description:
Projects like this are the heartbeat of our firm.

We’re incredibly honored to have been awarded the Georgetown Port Redevelopment Master Plan, helping shape the future of one of the most significant waterfront redevelopment opportunities in the Lowcountry.

For us, this project is about more than redevelopment. It’s about honoring Georgetown’s history, listening to the community, and creating a waterfront destination that feels authentic to the people who call it home. Our goal is to help shape

items.description: Not every downtown restaurant takes Mondays off! If you’re looking for a place to grab lunch, dinner, or a quick bite to start the week, here are some of the downtown spots you can count on for a Monday mood boost: 🫐Health in Hand 🍕Venus Pie Pizzeria 🍀Delaney's Irish Kitchen & Pub - Spartanburg 🍣Pokenori 🍝Sophia’s 🥨Main Street Pub Not a bad way to start a week if we do say so ourselves! 🤩🍴 #downtownspartanburg #spartanburgsc #experiencespartanburg

items.description: Congratulations to Wayne Butler on receiving the 2026 Outstanding Mentor Award from  👏 Wayne has spent years helping students explore careers in architecture, engineering, and construction through mentorship, leadership, and encouragement. As a mentor, board member, and scholarship advocate, he has made a lasting impact on students across the Charleston region.
